title: The Macro Minute: Are investors sleepwalking into another GFC?

Happy Monday out there team 42. It's
your skipper here, Darius Dale, to
present our macro minute for Monday,
April 6th, 2026. Hope everyone had a
wonderful Easter weekend with their
families. So, as always, we start with
the executive summary from today's lead
off morning note. So, let's dive right
in. Today's key macro question is, are
investors sleepwalking into another
global financial crisis?
Our answer is the risk of of this
outcome is low because it is hard for us
to envision such a tragedy given the
risk to the administration in the
mid-term and the 2028 elections. That
said, the longer the Iran conflict
persists, the longer the current global
liquidity crisis will persist. Moreover,
the latest forward guidance suggests
there will not likely be a major central
bank put in response to the current
global liquidity crisis. This implies
the current risk off inflation market
regime could devolve into a global
financial crisis if the Strait of Hormuz
remains effectively closed for months
and increasingly disrupts global dollar
recycling flows from net international
investment surplus Gulf Coast and Asian
economies.
Here are the most important things we
learned today regarding the six key
macro cycles that influence the momentum
and dispersion within and across asset
markets on a trend basis, starting with
the growth cycle. While the March ISM
Services PMI report did not support our
paradigm C theme, it strongly supported
our jobless recovery theme. Elsewhere,
the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively
closed from the perspective of the
global economy. The restricted crude oil
supply alone represents a historic
negative demand shock that could cost at
least 6 to 8% and upwards of 8 to 11% of
global GDP.
FYI, the Great Recession and Global
Financial Crisis corresponded to a 9%
decline in global GDP.
Let me say that again. The restricted
crude oil supply alone represents a
historic negative demand shock that
could cost at least 6 to 8% and upwards
of 8 to 11% of global GDP. FYI, the
Great Recession and Global Financial
Crisis corresponded to a 9% decline in
global GDP.
In terms of the inflation cycle, the
nascent energy supply shock represents a
boiling a pot of boiling water being
doused upon an otherwise cooling
inflation cycle and supports our sticky
inflation theme.
The latest is that Pakistan, Egypt, and
Turkey are actively negotiating a 45-day
ceasefire to preempt threatened US
strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure
and anticipated retaliation by the
Islamic Republic upon introducing a new
Tuesday 8:00 p.m. Oh, sorry. Sorry, my
apologies.
Let me read that over. Pakistan, Egypt,
and Turkey are actively negotiating a
45-day ceasefire to preempt threatened
US strikes on Iran's energy
infrastructure and anticipated
retaliation by the Islamic Republic upon
sensitive sites throughout the region.
In a profanity-laden truth social post
on Sunday, President Trump threatened to
destroy Iran's power plants and blow up
quote everything over there while
introducing a new Tuesday 8:00 p.m.
deadline for the Strait of Hormuz to be
reopened without specifying terms.
This latest delayed deadline extends a
sequence of delays since its first
threat on March 21st.
Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesman
Esmaeil Baghaei stated Tehran seeks a
permanent end to the conflict rather
than a temporary pause, asserting that
accepting a short-term detente without
guarantees is something that quote no
rational person would do.
Israeli Security Cabinet Minister Ze'ev
Elkin told Kan Radio on Monday that
outcomes involving a quote very dramatic
delay to Iran's nuclear program and
removal of enriched uranium would be
positive, but emphasized it doesn't look
like the Iranians are there.
Fars News Agency reports 15 vessels
transited the Strait of Hormuz with
Iranian approval over the past 24 hours.
I'm guessing they all went to China,
representing a 90% collapse relative to
pre-conflict volumes with no disclosure
on ownership or destinations. The
nascent directional trend is positive.
We're going from zero to 15, but the
cumulative impact of the lost petroleum
products has yet to be adequately priced
into global energy markets or felt by
the global economy. Recall that we have
a lot of
re-routing and SPR releases around the
world that will either eventually run
out or be turned off if these countries
really start to fear that they're going
to run out of energy. They're not just
going to be willy-nilly releasing SPRs
just to do regular old business cycle
activity. They're going to start to
ration energy products if this persists.
Uh mark my words on that. Uh the nascent
direction of I'm sorry, it's only a
matter of time before they do should the
conflict continue in terms of uh the the
adequate pricing
of this conflict and the restricted
supply into global energy markets and
the global economy in terms of the
impact that it will have on the global
economy in GDP terms. Uh
so it's only a matter of time that we
start to see those negative outcomes.
Elsewhere, the March ISM Services PMI
strongly supported our sticky inflation
theme. Nothing actual with respect to
our Bayesian priors for the monetary
policy cycle, the fiscal policy cycle,
liquidity cycle, or positioning cycle.
I'll continue reviewing and learn
whether our institutional grade risk
management overlays, KISS and Dr. Mo,
are evolving their signals in response
to these anticipated fundamental
dynamics. So as always a wrap up with

of that, and then update our models. So,

presentations are.

building my own custom macro dashboard
and sentiment models prior to joining 42
macro, and I spent an insane amount of

correlations, macro causations,

create these types of models and an

so clearly.

it's appreciated. So really super kind
thankful for this feedback.
You know, one of the things I'm super
passionate about
in this in this industry is, you know,
basically being the the counter to all
the weaponization of of your and and the
general investing public's cognitive
biases that I see out there.
There's so many folks who do what I do
who are smart enough to understand what
those cognitive biases are, what these
behavioral heuristics are and they
actually use them against you in their
marketing materials. They use them
against you, they weaponize them against
you when they're trying to acquire you
as a customer. They weaponize them
against you when they're trying to
maintain your uh subscription fees.
I've seen it for a long time throughout
my career up close and personal. This
There are people out there that are
literally will prey on you and your
inability to recognize your cognitive
biases. The same cognitive biases that
made Danny Kahneman and Amos Tversky and
Richard Thaler and all these other
people world famous Nobel Prize winning
researcher
investors. The same people There are a
lot of people who do what I do who will
that information and instead of
enhancing their process with this
information and and and using it to
build guardrails around their risk
management investment process, they'll
just take the information and use it to
create marketing materials so that they
can keep getting your subscription fees.
And that to me is it's gross, it's sick,
I I can't stand it. I've seen it up
close and personal for way too long and
I'm I'm going to our company exists to
combat that. And we're going to continue
to combat that. And one of the things
that I see out there that those folks
out there still doing is hiding behind
black boxes.
We publish all of our data.
If if if we have a model, you will be
able to backtest it, study it. There's
all the data is all available in every
single report we put out. You can
download the data, play with it,
backtest it, build your own tools with
it.
It's it's To me it's it's
If you're so good at investing, why
would you ever hide behind a black box?
It's I mean
The people who It should be hiding
behind black box working Man Group and
AQR and and Renaissance Technologies.
They don't sell emails for a living.
They don't sell signals for a living.
If you're If your model, whatever you
call your model is, is so good, why is
it a black box?
Why aren't you running billions of
dollars of AUM instead of sending out
emails? And that's the point I'm making
is obviously it's not it's not what they
reported to be.
Nobody selling emails should be hiding
behind a black box. It's an outrageous
activity. Nobody on the buy side
respects that black box. It's just the
people who the gullible retail investors
are getting their mickles tickled on a

Street, which is we spent about 30 or 40
minutes at the end of that that second
Around the Horn presentation on
behavioral heuristics, on cognitive
biases, on why we our process is built
to essentially remove the influence of
those cognitive biases from our
investing results. All of the best
investors that I've, you know,
interacted with and and studied with and
collaborated with and built models for
and had models stress tested by all of
those folks, weave these types of
solutions into their investment
processes to restrict the ability of
their cognitive biases to create
negative outcomes in their portfolio.
